summary: I read both guides (`guides/backend-guide.md` and `guides/ui-style-guide.md`) and the three iteration-3 Define-phase artifacts under review (`requirements.md` R1–R34, `open_questions.md`, `discovery_notes.md`), then checked the work against the guides' service/Result patterns, thin-controller and model rules, Tailwind scale, semantic status colors, Turbo conventions, and testing expectations. Because the Define phase produces plain-language business requirements and discovery notes — no code, design, or diff — the code-structural rules have nothing concrete to bind against yet and correctly defer to the design/build phases; where the specs do touch guide-governed surfaces they align rather than deviate (R4 and R26 require paused/failure states be shown "using a label and not color alone," honoring the UI accessibility baseline; R31 mandates plain non-technical language; the new R12 mirrors R16's in-progress treatment and the existing step-card live-progress/aria-live precedent), and the discovery notes map every proposed capability onto existing service POROs rather than callbacks, fat controllers, or model business logic. I found no blockers or deviations and wrote a **pass** verdict (empty findings) to `verdict.json`, carrying forward one non-blocking observation that R12/R16's in-progress indicators don't restate the label-not-color rule the way R4/R26 do, though they describe a progress indicator rather than a status color.
